title: session.go Session.TickStatus: defer unlock + panic recovery
description:
File: internal/session/session.go, lines 716-720

Session.TickStatus() runs as a bare goroutine (go s.TickStatus(stopStatus), line 544 in RunDaemon). It ticks every 1 second to re-render status bars for all connected clients via ForEachClient. The VT mutex is locked/unlocked manually each tick.

Current risk: If ForEachClient or RenderStatusBar() panics, the goroutine dies, the mutex stays locked (deadlocking the session), and status bars stop updating for all clients. No recover, not relaunched.

Fix:
1. Convert manual vt.Mu.Unlock() to defer-based unlock (wrap in anonymous func).
2. Add a recover() inside the for-loop tick case so a single bad render doesn't kill the ticker — it should log the panic and continue ticking.
